← Image Tools

Image Info Checker

Upload any image to instantly see its width, height, file size, format, aspect ratio and megapixel count.

🔍

Drop your image here

Upload any image to see its dimensions and details.

🔒 Your files never leave your device.

How to check image dimensions and file size

Upload your image and all metadata appears instantly — no button clicks needed. You'll see the exact pixel width and height, the file size in KB or MB, the image format, the aspect ratio and the total megapixel count. Results appear in under a second, all locally in your browser.

What information does the checker show?

Width and height in pixels, file size (raw and formatted), image format (JPG, PNG, WebP, GIF etc.), aspect ratio expressed as a simplified fraction (e.g. 16:9), and megapixels. This is all the information you need to decide whether an image meets the requirements for a website, social media platform, print job or email attachment.

Why do image dimensions matter?

Every platform has specific image requirements. Social networks crop or reject images that are the wrong aspect ratio. Website templates require images within certain file size limits for performance. Print shops need images above a minimum pixel density (typically 300 DPI) to avoid blurry output. Knowing your image's exact dimensions lets you make informed decisions before uploading or submitting.

What is an aspect ratio?

Aspect ratio is the proportional relationship between an image's width and height, expressed as two numbers (e.g. 16:9, 4:3, 1:1). A 1920×1080 image has a 16:9 ratio. A 1080×1080 image has a 1:1 ratio (square). Understanding aspect ratio helps you choose the right crop and avoid distortion when placing images into fixed-ratio containers.

Is my image safe?

Yes. Your image is read locally by the browser — it is never uploaded to any server. All metadata is extracted using browser APIs without any network requests.
